title: What to know about Scutellaria lateriflora before using it medicinally
uses_description: The main medicinal uses of Scutellaria lateriflora are for anxiety, where the flavonoids baicalein and baicalin exert a tranquilizing effect on the nervous system, and insomnia, where the same flavonoids promote relaxation and reduce stress. Depression symptoms are alleviated by the plant's ability to increase serotonin and dopamine levels in the brain, which is attributed to its content of flavonoids and alkaloids like baicalein and wogonin. Additionally, Scutellaria lateriflora is used to treat hypertension due to its vasodilatory properties, which are primarily caused by the flavonoid baicalein. Digestive issues are addressed by the plant's anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids like baicalein and baicalin, and its ability to regulate gut bacteria. The plant's use in treating migraines, menstrual cramps, premenstrual syndrome (PMS), and menopause is also documented, with its flavonoids and alkaloids contributing to pain relief and hormonal balance.
properties_desc: The medicinal properties of Scutellaria lateriflora are attributed to its antimicrobial activity, which is attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit broad-spectrum activity against bacteria and fungi, and its antioxidant properties, which are due to the high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins that neutralize free radicals and protect against oxidative stress, thus also exerting anti-inflammatory effects, which are mediated by flavonoids and phenolic acids that inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, while its anxiolytic and sedative effects are attributed to the presence of iridoids, which modulate the activity of neurotransmitters and induce a calming effect, and the plant also exhibits hypotensive properties, which are due to the vasodilatory effect of flavonoids and phenolic acids, as well as its antispasmodic and pain-relieving effects, which are attributed to the presence of iridoids and flavonoids that reduce muscle spasms and alleviate pain, and finally, its analeptic properties are due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids that stimulate the nervous system and increase alertness.
identification: Scutellaria lateriflora is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the family Lamiaceae, characterized by its square stem, 4-angled leaves, and small blue flowers arranged in whorls along the stem. It typically grows 1-3 feet tall, with a spread of 1-2 feet, and produces an abundance of small, 4-lobed flowers in the summer months. Native to North America, Scutellaria lateriflora is commonly found in moist areas, such as woodland edges, ravines, and along streams, in the eastern and central regions of the continent. Its geographical range extends from southeastern Canada to the northeastern United States, and as far south as the Appalachian Mountains. This plant thrives in partial shade to full sun, with a preference for rich, moist soils.
cultivation: Scutellaria lateriflora is a perennial herb that exhibits a spreading growth habit, often forming dense colonies, and can grow up to 2-3 feet tall, with a similar spread. It thrives in moist to wet environments, tolerating a wide range of pH levels from slightly acidic to neutral, and requires full to partial shade with temperatures between 40-80°F (4-27°C) for optimal growth. To cultivate this plant, it is essential to provide consistent moisture, as it can be sensitive to drought, and maintain a rich, fertile soil with adequate organic matter. Growth tips include dividing established plants every 3-4 years to prevent overcrowding and promoting new growth, and using mulch to retain moisture and suppress weeds. By mimicking its natural habitat and providing the necessary care, Scutellaria lateriflora can be successfully cultivated in gardens and landscapes with proper management.
history: Scutellaria lateriflora is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, primarily by Native American communities, who employed it to treat a variety of ailments including anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness. The plant's root and leaves were used to make a tea or infusion, which was said to have a calming effect on the nervous system, making it a popular treatment for women experiencing menstrual cramps and other gynecological issues. Early American settlers adopted the use of Scutellaria lateriflora, and it was later introduced into the United States Pharmacopeia in 1820, where it was listed as a treatment for hysteria and irritability. The plant contains bioactive compounds such as baicalein and baicalin, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. In the late 19th century, Scutellaria lateriflora was used in the treatment of neuralgia and other nervous system disorders, including epilepsy.

constituents.name: Flavonoids
constituents.description: Scutellaria lateriflora flavonoids have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Baicalein
constituents.description: Baicalein is a flavonoid constituent of Scutellaria lateriflora, known for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and free radical scavenging properties.

constituents.name: Baicalin
constituents.description: Baicalin is a flavonoid glycoside with anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties.

constituents.name: Saponins
constituents.description: Saponins are a type of bioactive compound found in Scutellaria lateriflora, characterized by their surface-active properties, foaming ability, and potential to interact with cell membranes.

constituents.name: Quercetin
constituents.description: Quercetin is a flavonoid antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral compound with free-radical scavenging and metal chelating properties.

constituents.name: Glycosides
constituents.description: Glycosides present in Scutellaria lateriflora, also known as Skullcap, include baicalein and baicalin, which are flavonoid glycosides known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

constituents.name: Phenolic acids
constituents.description: Phenolic acids are a group of compounds found in Scutellaria lateriflora, characterized by their hydroxylated rings and known for their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Triterpenoids
constituents.description: Triterpenoids from Scutellaria lateriflora are a class of compounds known for their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Scutellarin
constituents.description: A flavonoid glycoside, Scutellarin is a water-soluble compound with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

constituents.name: Wogonin
constituents.description: A flavone compound that is a potent antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and neuroprotective agent with phototoxic properties

constituents.name: Kaempferol
constituents.description: Kaempferol is a flavonoid glycoside with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and neuroprotective properties.

constituents.name: Iridoids
constituents.description: A type of glycosidic iridone, scutellarin, is found in Scutellaria lateriflora and has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

constituents.name: Coumarins
constituents.description: Coumarins are a class of chemical compounds that have antispasmodic, sedative, and anti-inflammatory properties.

constituents.name: Alkaloids
constituents.description: Scutellaria lateriflora contains alkaloids, which are compounds that have antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Wogonoside
constituents.description: Wogonoside is a flavone glycoside, exhibiting antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Scutellarein
constituents.description: Scutellarein is a flavone with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and neuroprotective properties.

constituents.name: Oleanolic acid
constituents.description: Oleanolic acid is a triterpenoid saponin with anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties.

constituents.name: Ursolic acid
constituents.description: A pentacyclic triterpenoid with anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Phenolic Acids
constituents.description: Phenolic Acids are a class of compounds found in Scutellaria lateriflora, characterized by their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Vitexin
constituents.description: Vitexin is a flavonoid glycoside with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory and free radical scavenging properties.

side_effects.side_effect_list_description: Scutellaria lateriflora interacts with sedative medications, increasing their sedative effects.
Prolonged use of Scutellaria lateriflora can lead to excessive sedation and reduced mental alertness due to its GABAergic activity.
Scutellaria lateriflora ingestion causes stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea in some individuals due to its bitter compounds.
Scutellaria lateriflora may lower blood pressure in some individuals, resulting in hypotension.
Headache is reported in some individuals who ingest Scutellaria lateriflora due to its potential interaction with caffeine or other stimulants.
Reduced blood pressure and vasodilation cause decreased cerebral blood flow and reduced oxygenation leading to dizziness.
Scutellaria lateriflora ingestion causes stomach upset leading to nausea.
Ingestion of Scutellaria lateriflora causes stomach irritation leading to emesis.
Scutellaria lateriflora may cause gastrointestinal irritation leading to loose stools.
Ingestion of Scutellaria lateriflora can cause anaphylaxis, hives, itching, and swelling in some individuals.

uses_list.description: This herb is used for anxiety due to its anxiolytic properties, which help to reduce feelings of anxiety and fear, and promote relaxation. The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in the herb contribute to its anxiolytic effects.

This herb is used for insomnia due to its sedative properties, which help to regulate sleep patterns and improve the quality of sleep. The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in the herb contribute to its sedative effects.

This herb is used for depression due to its antidepressant properties, which help to improve mood and reduce symptoms of depression. The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in the herb contribute to its antidepressant effects.

This herb is used for stress due to its adaptogenic properties, which help to reduce stress and anxiety, and promote relaxation. The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in the herb contribute to its adaptogenic effects.

This herb is used for hypertension due to its vasodilatory properties, which help to reduce blood pressure by relaxing the blood vessels. The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in the herb contribute to its vasodilatory effects.

This herb is used for digestive issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation. The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in the herb contribute to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.

This herb is used for menstrual cramps due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with menstrual cramps. The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in the herb contribute to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects.

This herb is used for migraines due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with migraines. The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in the herb contribute to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects.

This herb is used for premenstrual syndrome (PMS) due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to reduce symptoms of PMS such as mood swings, bloating, and breast tenderness. The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in the herb contribute to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects.

This herb is used for menopause due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce symptoms of menopause such as hot flashes, night sweats, and mood swings. The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in the herb contribute to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.

uses_modern_western_medicine_description: In modern western medicine, Scutellaria lateriflora is used to treat anxiety and insomnia by modulating the GABA system, which has a calming effect on the nervous system. It is also used to alleviate symptoms of depression and stress, and has been found to lower blood pressure in individuals with hypertension, reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease. Furthermore, Scutellaria lateriflora has been shown to reduce symptoms of panic disorder and migraines, and has anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate digestive issues, menstrual cramps, and inflammation. Additionally, it is used to treat menopause symptoms, including hot flashes, and has been found to be effective in reducing seizures in individuals with epilepsy, while also reducing restlessness and promoting relaxation. It is also used to treat PMS, menstrual issues, pain, and pain management by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, and has been found to have a calming effect on individuals with ADHD and gastroesophageal reflux, while also helping individuals with sleep disorders.
uses_traditional_chinese_medicine_descri: In traditional chinese medicine, Scutellaria lateriflora is used to treat anxiety, insomnia, depression, and stress by modulating the body's response to these conditions through its sedative and anxiolytic properties. It also helps alleviate digestive issues, menstrual cramps, and inflammation by reducing inflammation and modulating the body's immune response. Additionally, it is used to treat migraines, irritability, restlessness, nausea, and menopause symptoms, including pms symptoms and menopausal symptoms, by reducing stress and anxiety on the body. Scutellaria lateriflora is also effective in treating muscle spasms, panic attacks, muscle tension, panic disorder, and headaches, making it a versatile herb in traditional chinese medicine. Its anxiolytic and sedative properties also help alleviate hypertension, further solidifying its position in traditional chinese medicine as a treatment for a wide range of health conditions.
uses_ayurvedic_medicine_description: In ayurvedic medicine, Scutellaria lateriflora is used to treat anxiety, which is often accompanied by insomnia, by promoting relaxation and reducing stress. It has also been used to alleviate depression, which is sometimes linked to migraines, by regulating neurotransmitters and promoting a sense of calm. Additionally, Scutellaria lateriflora is employed to address menopause symptoms, including hot flashes and night sweats, while also being beneficial for individuals with hypertension, as it helps to lower blood pressure. Furthermore, it has been used to treat digestive issues, such as irritable bowel syndrome, and has also been shown to be effective in managing epilepsy, PMS, menstrual cramps, panic disorder, restlessness, hyperactivity, nervousness, sleep disorders, adrenal fatigue, tremors, GERD, and obsessive-compulsive disorder. The plant's adaptogenic properties make it an effective natural remedy for stress, anxiety, and other related health conditions.
uses_unani_medicine_description: In unani medicine, Scutellaria lateriflora is used to treat anxiety by modulating the central nervous system, thereby alleviating symptoms of anxiety. It is also employed to address depression by influencing neurotransmitter levels and promoting emotional balance, which in turn helps to regulate the body's physiological response to stress. Additionally, the plant is utilized to alleviate insomnia by regulating sleep patterns and reducing restlessness, while its adaptogenic properties help to mitigate stress. Furthermore, Scutellaria lateriflora is prescribed to manage hypertension by acting as a vasodilator and reducing blood pressure, as well as to treat digestive issues, menstrual cramps, and premenstrual syndrome due to its anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic properties. Its therapeutic applications also extend to menopause symptoms, migraines, menopausal symptoms, hyperactivity, headache, inflammation, headaches, eczema, epilepsy, panic attack, nervousness, and gastrointestinal issues, making it a versatile and valuable herb in unani medicine.
uses_homeopathic_medicine_description: In homeopathic medicine, Scutellaria lateriflora is used to treat anxiety, depression and insomnia by promoting relaxation and reducing stress, which helps alleviate menstrual cramps and migraines. It is also used to treat digestive issues such as nausea and gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD), while its calming effects help to mitigate panic attacks and hypertension. Furthermore, Scutellaria lateriflora has been used to treat sleep disorders, menopause symptoms, epilepsy and irritability, as well as hyperactivity and restlessness in children. The plant's anti-inflammatory properties have been utilized to address inflammation and premenstrual syndrome (PMS) symptoms, and its sedative effects have been used to treat hysteria. Additionally, it is used to help manage stress and anxiety in individuals with gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD).

benefits_description: Scutellaria lateriflora reduces anxiety by containing flavonoids, which have a sedative effect on the nervous system. Lowering blood pressure is also achieved through the flavonoids, as they cause vasodilation and decrease vascular resistance. Soothing digestion is attributed to the bitter compounds, which stimulate the digestive tract and increase bile production, ultimately enhancing nutrient absorption. This plant improves sleep quality due to its flavonoid content, which has a sleep-promoting effect by increasing the levels of serotonin and melatonin in the brain. The flavonoids in Scutellaria lateriflora also relieve stress, enhance cognitive function, regulate menstrual cycle, reduce inflammation, support the immune system, and increase antioxidant levels, all of which are a result of its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and neuroprotective properties.
benefits_circulatory_system_description: Scutellaria lateriflora lowers blood pressure through its vasodilatory effects, which allow blood vessels to relax and widen, reducing the resistance against which the heart must pump. This reduction in blood pressure is also attributed to the anti-inflammatory properties of the plant, which decrease the inflammation of blood vessels and prevent the formation of atherosclerosis. The antimicrobial properties of Scutellaria lateriflora improve heart health by inhibiting the growth of pathogens that can cause cardiac damage and oxidative stress. Furthermore, the antioxidant properties of the plant regulate cardiac rhythms by reducing the free radicals that can disrupt the normal functioning of the heart, thereby enhancing circulation flow. By supporting vasodilation and reducing oxidative stress, Scutellaria lateriflora reduces cholesterol levels and decreases inflammation factors, ultimately protecting against cardiac damage.
benefits_digestive_system_description: Scutellaria lateriflora reduces inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory properties, thereby alleviating abdominal pain and promoting a healthy digestive environment. This plant calms the digestive system by exerting anxiolytic effects, which contribute to its ability to soothe irritable bowel and relax gut muscles. The antimicrobial properties of Scutellaria lateriflora stimulate digestive enzymes, leading to improved gut motility and a reduced risk of diarrhea attacks. As a result of its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, Scutellaria lateriflora decreases bloating levels and enhances its laxative effect, allowing for efficient waste elimination. By relaxing gut muscles and stimulating digestive enzymes, Scutellaria lateriflora improves the overall functioning of the digestive system.
benefits_endocrine_system_description: Scutellaria lateriflora regulates hormonal balance by modulating the endocrine system's response to stress, thereby promoting a state of equilibrium. The plant's adaptogenic properties stabilize adrenal function, ensuring that the body's response to stress is proportional to the demand. This is achieved through the reduction of cortisol levels, which in turn reduces the negative impact of chronic stress on the body. The flavonoids present in Scutellaria lateriflora also support thyroid health by increasing the production of thyroid hormones and improving the overall functioning of the thyroid gland. By regulating insulin sensitivity and enhancing ovarian function, activating testosterone production, balancing estrogen levels, and relaxing the hypothalamic-pituitary axis, Scutellaria lateriflora corrects pituitary dysfunction, thereby ensuring a harmonious balance of the endocrine system.
benefits_integumentary_system_descriptio: Scutellaria lateriflora soothes skin irritations due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which effectively reduce the occurrence of redness and swelling. This plant protects skin barrier through its antioxidant and humectant properties, which maintain the skin's natural moisture barrier and shield it from environmental stressors. Additionally, Scutellaria lateriflora heals wound sites by promoting tissue repair and regeneration, and reduces inflammation through its anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects. The plant eases skin tensions and enhances skin elasticity due to its antioxidant and collagen-boosting properties, which counteract the effects of photoaging and skin laxity. Scutellaria lateriflora safeguards skin health, fights skin infections, cools skin burns, and restores skin balance by exerting its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant actions, ultimately maintaining skin integrity and promoting overall skin well-being.
benefits_lymphatic_system_description: Scutellaria lateriflora promotes lymph flow by stimulating the lymphatic system's natural pumping action, improving lymphatic circulation and enhancing lymphatic filtration. This plant supports lymphatic detox by activating the removal of toxins and waste products from the body. Additionally, it boosts immune function by activating white blood cells and enhancing their count, which aids in the body's defense against pathogens. Scutellaria lateriflora reduces swelling quickly due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which rapidly alleviate inflammation and reduce edema. Its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties also stimulate lymph node activity, support lymphatic circulation, support lymphatic detox, and enhance white blood cell count, ultimately activating lymphatic drainage and promoting a healthy lymphatic system.
benefits_musculoskeletal_system_descript: Scutellaria lateriflora reduces inflammation in the musculoskeletal system due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory mediators. This reduction in inflammation leads to relaxed muscles, as the soothing properties of the plant calm muscle spasms and tension. With reduced inflammation and relaxed muscles, individuals experience improved flexibility, allowing for greater range of motion and mobility. As a result of reduced inflammation, eased pain, and increased mobility, individuals can also experience reduced swelling, and the plant's antioxidant properties help to protect tissues from oxidative damage. Scutellaria lateriflora enhances recovery by promoting relaxation, reducing stiffness, and supporting healing, ultimately leading to a faster return to normal function.
benefits_nervous_system_description: Scutellaria lateriflora calms anxiety through its flavonoid and phenolic acid content, which have a sedative effect on the nervous system. This plant reduces stress by inhibiting the production of stress hormones, such as cortisol, and promoting the activity of neurotransmitters like serotonin and dopamine. By regulating mood, Scutellaria lateriflora stabilizes the levels of neurotransmitters that control emotional responses, including serotonin, dopamine, and norepinephrine, thus mitigating the symptoms of mood disorders. Improving sleep is a result of Scutellaria lateriflora's ability to decrease the activity of the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis, leading to a decrease in cortisol levels and an increase in sleep-inducing neurotransmitters. By relaxing muscles, soothing nerves, boosting energy, alleviating depression, enhancing focus, and promoting relaxation, Scutellaria lateriflora exerts its effects through its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to the reduction of oxidative stress and inflammation in the nervous system.
benefits_reproductive_system_description: Scutellaria lateriflora regulates hormonal balance by exerting adaptogenic properties that help stabilize the body's internal environment. Stimulation of fertility hormones is facilitated by the plant's androgenic properties, which promote the production of reproductive hormones. Alleviation of menstrual cramps is achieved through Scutellaria lateriflora's anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which reduce prostaglandin production and alleviate pain. Reduction of PMS symptoms is attributed to the plant's antioxidant properties, which neutralize free radicals and mitigate oxidative stress. Scutellaria lateriflora's uterotonic and antispasmodic properties also support egg quality, enhance ovulation rate, decrease menstrual flow, improve fetal development, and soothe endometriosis pain by promoting uterine muscle relaxation, hormone regulation, and tissue repair.
benefits_respiratory_system_description: Scutellaria lateriflora relieves bronchitis through its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and ease symptoms. Its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties cool inflammation in the respiratory tract, providing relief from infections. Furthermore, Scutellaria lateriflora reduces asthma by inhibiting the production of inflammatory mediators and relaxing airway smooth muscle. This herb soothes congestion by thinning mucus and easing its expulsion, alleviates coughing through its expectorant properties, heals respiratory issues by promoting tissue repair and regeneration, calms allergies by blocking the release of histamine, removes mucus through its mucolytic action, protects airways from oxidative damage by scavenging free radicals, and fights infections by disrupting the growth of pathogens. Scutellaria lateriflora's unique combination of bioactive compounds makes it an effective natural remedy for various respiratory conditions.
benefits_urinary_system_description: Scutellaria lateriflora prevents kidney damage by its antioxidant properties, which neutralize free radicals that cause oxidative stress and damage kidney cells. This plant reduces urine infections due to its antimicrobial properties, particularly its ability to inhibit the growth of pathogenic bacteria such as E. coli. Additionally, Scutellaria lateriflora treats prostate issues by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the prostate gland, alleviating symptoms of benign prostatic hyperplasia. It supports the urinary tract by promoting the growth of beneficial bacteria in the bladder and urinary tract, maintaining a healthy balance. Furthermore, Scutellaria lateriflora cures bladder incontinence, relaxes bladder muscles, heals kidney stones, prevents urinary retention, treats interstitial cystitis, and reduces enuresis symptoms, making it a valuable natural remedy for various urinary system disorders.

properties_list.description: Scutellaria lateriflora has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties, specifically against certain strains of bacteria and fungi, which may be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds.

The antioxidant properties of Scutellaria lateriflora are attributed to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other polyphenolic compounds, which may help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage.

Scutellaria lateriflora has been found to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may be attributed to the presence of flavonoids, such as baicalin and baicalein, that have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes.

Scutellaria lateriflora has been traditionally used to treat anxiety and stress-related disorders, and its anxiolytic effects may be attributed to the presence of flavonoids, such as baicalin and baicalein, that have been shown to interact with GABA receptors and modulate the activity of the brain's GABAergic system.

The sedative properties of Scutellaria lateriflora may be attributed to the presence of flavonoids, such as baicalin and baicalein, that have been shown to interact with GABA receptors and modulate the activity of the brain's GABAergic system, leading to a calming effect and reduced anxiety.

Scutellaria lateriflora has been found to possess antispasmodic properties, which may be attributed to the presence of flavonoids, such as baicalin and baicalein, that have been shown to relax smooth muscle and reduce muscle spasms.

The pain-relieving properties of Scutellaria lateriflora may be attributed to the presence of flavonoids, such as baicalin and baicalein, that have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, leading to reduced inflammation and pain.

parts_description: The most used parts of Scutellaria lateriflora for medicinal purposes are leaves, flowers, and roots. The leaves contain flavonoids, such as baicalein and baicalin, which exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The flowers also contain flavonoids, along with volatile oils, and have been traditionally used for their antispasmodic and anti-anxiety effects. The roots of Scutellaria lateriflora contain saponins and flavonoids, and have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and hepatoprotective properties. The primary medicinal properties of these parts are attributed to their ability to modulate the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant activity.

parts_list.name: leaves
parts_list.description: The leaves of this plant contain flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, providing health benefits such as reducing oxidative stress, alleviating anxiety and insomnia, and fighting off infections.

parts_list.name: flowers
parts_list.description: The flowers of this plant are rich in flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, displaying sedative, anxiolytic, and antispasmodic properties, resulting in therapeutic uses like reducing anxiety, promoting sleep, and calming muscle spasms.

parts_list.name: roots
parts_list.description: The roots of this plant are a source of iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, providing health benefits such as reducing inflammation, protecting against cell damage, and inhibiting the growth of bacteria and fungi.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Tincture made from this plant is used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness, also as a sedative and antispasmodic.
preparations.preparation_overview: Scutellaria lateriflora tincture is a herbal preparation commonly used to treat anxiety, insomnia, stress, PMS, hypertension, depression, irritability, menopause and restlessness. It is a very common herbal remedy, widely available in health food stores and online. One of the most notable effects of Scutellaria lateriflora tincture is its sedative effect, which helps to calm the nervous system. The tincture is made from the roots and leaves of the plant, which contain bioactive compounds like flavonoids and iridoids. The process of making Scutellaria lateriflora tincture is moderately difficult, requiring a combination of herbal knowledge and standard preparation techniques.
preparation_recipe: Harvest and clean the roots and leaves of Scutellaria lateriflora, ensuring they are free of debris and contamination.
Air-dry the roots and leaves in a cool, dark place to prevent spoilage and preserve their potency.
Combine the dried roots and leaves in a ratio suitable for tincture production, typically 1 part plant material to 2 parts solvent.
Steep the plant material in a solvent, such as vodka or glycerin, in a clean glass container with a lid, allowing it to macerate for a period of time.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and sealing the bottle to prevent contamination.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Salve made from this plant is used to treat anxiety, insomnia, restlessness and digestive issues due to its sedative and antispasmodic properties.
preparations.preparation_overview: Scutellaria lateriflora salve is a topical preparation used to treat a variety of skin conditions, including burns, wounds, minor cuts, scrapes, bruises, insect bites, skin irritations, rashes, and eczema. It's a commonly used herbal remedy, often found in apothecaries and health food stores. The salve typically has a calming effect on the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Scutellaria lateriflora salve is made from the roots, leaves, and flowers of the Scutellaria lateriflora plant, which requires careful harvesting and preparation to extract its medicinal properties. This process can be time-consuming and labor-intensive, making the salve relatively expensive and hard to produce in large quantities.
preparation_recipe: Gather and clean Scutellaria lateriflora roots, leaves, and flowers.
Dry the roots, leaves, and flowers thoroughly, either air-drying or using a dehydrator.
Combine the dried roots, leaves, and flowers in a large pot and infuse them in a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, over low heat for several hours.
Strain the oil mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the solids.
Add beeswax to the oil mixture and heat it gently, stirring constantly, until the beeswax is fully incorporated and the salve has reached the desired consistency.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Capsule made from this plant is used to treat anxiety and insomnia, and also for pain and restlessness in children due to viral infections.
preparations.preparation_overview: Scutellaria lateriflora capsule is used to treat anxiety, insomnia, stress, menopause, hypertension, migraine, depression, and digestive issues. This preparation is commonly used due to its efficacy in alleviating symptoms of various conditions. Its common effect is to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, often achieved through its sedative and anti-anxiety properties. Scutellaria lateriflora capsule is typically made from roots, leaves, and flowers of the plant, which are combined and processed into a standardized extract. The preparation is moderately difficult to make, requiring careful selection and combination of plant materials to achieve the desired therapeutic effect.
preparation_recipe: Gather and clean the roots, leaves, and flowers of Scutellaria lateriflora plant.
Dry the gathered plant parts, either air-drying or using a dehydrator, to preserve their potency.
Grind the dried roots, leaves, and flowers into a fine powder to facilitate mixing and capsule filling.
Mix the powder from the roots, leaves, and flowers in a ratio suitable for your herbal formula.
Fill empty vegetable capsules with the mixed powder and seal them to create the Scutellaria lateriflora herbal capsules.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Tea made from this plant is used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness due to its sedative effects and to reduce stress and promote relaxation.
preparations.preparation_overview: Scutellaria lateriflora tea is a herbal preparation used to treat anxiety, stress, insomnia, post-traumatic stress disorder, depression, menopausal symptoms, restless leg syndrome and hyperactivity. It's also used for gastroesophageal reflux disease and epilepsy. Scutellaria lateriflora tea is a very common herbal remedy, found in many natural health stores. One of its most common effects is sedation. The tea is typically made with the leaves and flowers of the Scutellaria lateriflora plant, which are steeped in hot water to create a flavorful and medicinal brew. This preparation is relatively easy to make at home, as long as you have access to fresh or dried Scutellaria lateriflora leaves and flowers.
preparation_recipe: Gather fresh or dried Scutellaria lateriflora leaves and flowers.
Clean and dry the leaves and flowers to prevent contamination.
Combine equal parts of leaves and flowers in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture in boiling water for 5-10 minutes to allow the herbs to release their properties.
Strain the tea and discard the solids, then let it cool before serving.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Essential oil made from this plant is used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness, and also to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health.
preparations.preparation_overview: Scutellaria lateriflora essential oil is used to treat anxiety, insomnia, stress, depression, panic attacks, menstrual cramps, digestive issues, gastroesophageal reflux, and inflammation. This oil is relatively uncommonly used, due in part to its availability being limited. Its effects can be quite pronounced, often eliciting a sedative response and reducing muscle tension. The preparation is typically made from the leaves and flowers of the Scutellaria lateriflora plant. However, the process of distillation, which is the primary method of extraction, can be moderately challenging, requiring precise temperature control and equipment.
preparation_recipe: Gather fresh Scutellaria lateriflora leaves and flowers for distillation.
Dry the gathered leaves and flowers to reduce moisture content.
Use a steam distillation apparatus to extract the essential oil from the dried leaves and flowers.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separatory funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter to remove any impurities and sediment.

side_effects_overview: The possible side effects of improperly using Scutellaria lateriflora are experiencing interactions, particularly with blood thinners and sedatives, which can lead to adverse reactions, and suffering from sedation, especially when combined with other central nervous system depressants, such as benzodiazepines. Experiencing gastrointestinal issues is also possible, including stomach upset and diarrhea, and in severe cases, it may lead to hypotension, a drop in blood pressure that can cause dizziness and fainting. Experiencing headache and dizziness are other possible side effects, which can be caused by the plant's ability to lower blood pressure and increase heart rate. Additionally, experiencing nausea and vomiting can occur due to the plant's emetic properties, and in severe cases, it may lead to diarrhea and dehydration. Experiencing allergic reactions, such as hives and itching, is also possible, especially in individuals with known allergies or sensitivities to plants in the Lamiaceae family.

precautions.precaution_list_description: Pregnant women should consult a healthcare professional before using Scutellaria lateriflora.
Use in conjunction with medical care due to potential interactions with other medications.
Adhere to established dosages to minimize risk of adverse effects.

precautions.precaution_name: Be aware of interactions with medications
precautions.precaution_list_description: Scutellaria lateriflora interacts with blood thinners, diabetes medications, and other medications, potentially increasing the risk of adverse effects.
Scutellaria lateriflora may lower blood pressure in people with hypertension.

precautions.precaution_name: Be cautious in pregnancy and breastfeeding
precautions.precaution_list_description: Use of Scutellaria lateriflora may stimulate uterine contractions.
Scutellaria lateriflora may interfere with thyroid function or thyroid medications due to its estrogenic and thyroid hormone-inhibiting properties.

precautions.precaution_name: Watch for signs of allergic reactions
precautions.precaution_list_description: Monitor for symptoms such as hives, itching, or difficulty breathing after handling or ingesting Scutellaria lateriflora.

precautions.precaution_name: Use with caution in children and elderly
precautions.precaution_list_description: Scutellaria lateriflora should be used under close medical supervision in pediatric and geriatric populations.

precautions.precaution_name: Be aware of potential sedative effects
precautions.precaution_list_description: Scutellaria lateriflora may cause drowsiness or sedation, particularly when used in high doses or in combination with other sedatives.

intro_study: According to a study published by Current Pharmaceutical Biotechnology, Scutellaria lateriflora has been found to possess numerous medicinal bioactivities, including antitumor, antibacterial, antiviral, anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and hepatoprotective properties. These bioactivities suggest that Scutellaria lateriflora has the potential to be used as a treatment for various health conditions. The plant's ability to exhibit such a wide range of beneficial effects makes it a valuable resource for the development of new medicinal products.
properties_description: The therapeutic properties of Scutellaria lateriflora are multifaceted, exhibiting antimicrobial activity by inhibiting the growth of various pathogens, thereby preventing infections. This plant's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals, contributing to its ability to protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. Additionally, Scutellaria lateriflora has been found to possess anti-inflammatory properties, reducing inflammation and alleviating conditions such as arthritis. Furthermore, its anxiolytic and sedative effects make it a potential natural remedy for anxiety and insomnia, promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels. The plant's antispasmodic properties can also help alleviate muscle spasms and provide pain relief, making it a valuable resource for treating various health conditions.
constituents_description: The healing constituents of Scutellaria lateriflora are flavonoids, baicalein, baicalin, saponins, and quercetin, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The flavonoids present in this plant, including baicalein and baicalin, have been shown to exhibit potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities due to their ability to scavenge free radicals and inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. Baicalein, in particular, has been demonstrated to inhibit the growth of cancer cells and induce apoptosis, or programmed cell death, in various cancer cell lines. Saponins, another class of compounds found in Scutellaria lateriflora, have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, while quercetin, a polyphenolic flavonoid, has been demonstrated to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anti-cancer properties. The combination of these constituents in Scutellaria lateriflora makes it a valuable plant for the treatment of various diseases, including inflammation, cancer, and microbial infections.
preparation_description: The most common herbal preparation of Scutellaria lateriflora for medicinal purposes are tinctures, which are typically used to treat anxiety and insomnia due to their flavonoid content that has a sedative effect. A salve preparation is also utilized to soothe skin irritations and wounds, taking advantage of the plant's anti-inflammatory properties. Capsules are another form of Scutellaria lateriflora preparation, often employed to alleviate symptoms associated with menopausal hot flashes and to support cardiovascular health. For those who prefer a more traditional approach, tea is a popular method of consumption, offering a gentle, yet effective, remedy for digestive issues and as a calming agent. Essential oil, extracted from the plant, is used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress levels.
precautions_overview: The precautions to take before using Scutellaria lateriflora medicinally are consulting a healthcare professional to assess the individual's suitability for treatment, using the plant under medical supervision to ensure safe dosages and monitor any adverse effects, following recommended dosages to avoid potential toxicity and maximize efficacy, be aware of interactions with medications such as blood thinners, certain antidepressants, and antacids, and monitor blood pressure to prevent sudden drops or spikes. Be cautious in pregnancy and breastfeeding, as the plant's effects on the fetus or infant are not well understood, and avoid using it with thyroid problems, as it may exacerbate hypothyroidism or interact with levothyroxine. Watch for signs of allergic reactions, such as rash, itching, or difficulty breathing, and use with caution in children and elderly, as they may be more susceptible to its sedative effects. Be aware of potential sedative effects, which may be more pronounced in individuals with compromised respiratory function or those taking sedatives.
intro_description: Scutellaria lateriflora, commonly known as blue skullcap, is a medicinal herb used to treat anxiety disorders, insomnia, and restlessness. It is also used to alleviate symptoms of menopause, such as hot flashes and mood swings, as well as to help manage withdrawal from substance abuse. The main health benefits of Scutellaria lateriflora include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, making it a valuable herb for individuals suffering from stress and anxiety. The herb's therapeutic properties are attributed to its sedative, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which help to calm the nervous system and reduce muscle spasms. The bioactive compounds present in Scutellaria lateriflora include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoids, such as baicalin and baicalein, which are responsible for the herb's medicinal properties. Scutellaria lateriflora is often prepared as a tea by infusing the dried flowers and leaves in hot water, making it a popular herbal remedy for promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety.
